The docs of https://github.com/github/gh-aw says, for services: from the agent, connect via host.docker.internal, not localhost.

Help me to identify the root cause. Fix `host.docker.internal` issue in runner requirement.

input.name: Fix host.docker.internal routing
input.overview: Update gh-sr container-mode runner networking so AWF agent containers resolve `host.docker.internal` to the reachable AWF gateway path used for host-published service ports, then verify with focused tests and docs alignment.
input.plan:
# Fix host.docker.internal Routing for AWF Agent Containers

## Root Cause Confirmed
From run `25342374315` logs, AWF agent containers received `DATABASE_HOST=host.docker.internal` and attempted host-service access through a mapping that resolved to `172.18.0.1` (host-gateway path), while your audited reachable host-service gateway for sandbox traffic is `172.30.0.1`.

The current gh-sr runner image behavior that causes this is in [`/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh`](/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh), where `gh-aw-firewall/agent` runs are forced to:
- `--add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway`

That hard override can pin `host.docker.internal` to a non-reachable gateway for DB/Redis host ports in AWF sandbox runs.

## Implementation Plan

- Update AWF-agent host override in [`/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh`](/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh)
  - Replace the injected `host-gateway` mapping for `gh-aw-firewall/agent` with an AWF-gateway mapping compatible with container-mode host-service routing (default `172.30.0.1`).
  - Keep behavior scoped only to `gh-aw-firewall/agent` invocations and preserve existing duplicate-override protections.
  - Keep MCP gateway (`gh-aw-mcpg`) behavior unchanged.

- Add/adjust regression tests in [`/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/runner/container_test.go`](/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/runner/container_test.go)
  - Update wrapper argv tests that currently assert `host-gateway` injection so they assert the new AWF-gateway mapping.
  - Add a focused test for explicit existing `--add-host host.docker.internal:<ip>` passthrough to ensure no double-rewrite regressions.

- Align diagnostics and operator guidance text
  - Update comments/check guidance in [`/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/agentic/agentic.go`](/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/agentic/agentic.go) where host-gateway path is described for AWF agent connectivity.
  - Update docs in [`/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md) to reflect the new AWF-agent host mapping rationale for service-port reachability.

## Verification

- Run targeted unit tests:
  - `go test ./internal/runner -run DockerWrapper -count=1`
  - `go test ./internal/agentic -count=1`
- Run full package checks for confidence:
  - `go test ./...`
- Validate expected behavior in logs on next agentic run:
  - `gh-aw-firewall/agent` docker invocation should show `host.docker.internal` mapped to AWF gateway route.
  - Rails/Redis checks should no longer attempt `172.18.0.1:5432/6379` when host.docker.internal is used.

## Risk Watch

- Any environment where AWF gateway differs from `172.30.0.1` may need a configurable gateway value; if seen, follow up with a configurable env-driven mapping in wrapper/entrypoint.
- Keep MCP traffic path stable while changing DB/Redis host-service routing semantics.

todos.content: Change docker-wrapper AWF agent host override from host-gateway to AWF gateway mapping
Adjust/add container wrapper tests for new host mapping behavior
Update agentic diagnostics comments and docs to match new routing model
Execute targeted and full Go tests to verify no regressions

input.old_string:
# It also intercepts `docker run|create ... ghcr.io/github/gh-aw-firewall/agent:* ...`
# and injects `--add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway` when the caller did
# not already add an explicit `host.docker.internal` host entry. AWF agent
# containers often sit on a custom Docker network where inner DNS resolution of
# `host.docker.internal` can flake; Docker's host-gateway mapping is the stable
# route to the MCP gateway on the inner host network (port 80).
input.new_string:
# It also intercepts `docker run|create ... ghcr.io/github/gh-aw-firewall/agent:* ...`
# and injects `--add-host=host.docker.internal:<AWF bridge gateway>` when the caller did
# not already add an explicit `host.docker.internal` host entry. AWF agent containers
# sit on custom Docker networks; inner DNS for `host.docker.internal` can flake.
# Docker's `host-gateway` often maps to an inner-bridge IP (e.g. 172.18.0.1) that is
# not where AWF exposes host-published service ports — the AWF bridge gateway
# (default 172.30.0.1, same as MCP URL rewrite above) is the stable route for port 80,
# 5432, 6379, etc. Override with GH_SR_AWF_BRIDGE_GATEWAY_IP if your AWF layout differs.

real="${GH_SR_DOCKER_WRAPPER_REAL:-/usr/bin/docker}"

# AWF host-access / bridge gateway inside the inner Docker network (see gh-aw firewall
# allow-host-service-ports and MCP rewrite to 172.30.0.1 in this shim).
AWF_HOST_DOCKER_INTERNAL_IP="${GH_SR_AWF_BRIDGE_GATEWAY_IP:-172.30.0.1}"
needs_awf_agent_bridge_host() {

input.old_string:
if needs_awf_agent_host_gateway "$@"; then
    sub="$1"
    shift
    exec "$real" "$sub" --add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway "$@"
fi
input.new_string:
if needs_awf_agent_bridge_host "$@"; then
    sub="$1"
    shift
    exec "$real" "$sub" --add-host=host.docker.internal:"$AWF_HOST_DOCKER_INTERNAL_IP" "$@"
fi

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented:

### What changed
- **[`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh)** — For `ghcr.io/github/gh-aw-firewall/agent` `docker run`/`create`, the shim now adds `--add-host=host.docker.internal:"$AWF_HOST_DOCKER_INTERNAL_IP"` with `AWF_HOST_DOCKER_INTERNAL_IP="${GH_SR_AWF_BRIDGE_GATEWAY_IP:-172.30.0.1}"` (replacing `host-gateway`, which could resolve to e.g. `172.18.0.1` and break AWF host-published ports). Renamed predicate to `needs_awf_agent_bridge_host`.
- **[`internal/runner/container_test.go`](internal/runner/container_test.go)** — Expectations updated; added `awf_agent_respects_gateway_env_override`; embed test renamed to `TestAgenticRunnerDockerWrapperEmbedsAwfBridgeHostMapping`.
- **[`internal/agentic/agentic.go`](internal/agentic/agentic.go)** + **[`internal/agentic/container_network_test.go`](internal/agentic/container_network_test.go)** — Inner-network doctor probe mirrors the shim (`172.30.0.1`); remediation text updated.
- **[`internal/doctor/doctor.go`](internal/doctor/doctor.go)** — Success line text aligned with the new route.
- **[`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile)** + **[`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md)** — Comments/docs describe AWF bridge mapping and `GH_SR_AWF_BRIDGE_GATEWAY_IP`.

### What was verified
- `go test ./internal/runner -run DockerWrapper -count=1` — OK  
- `go test ./internal/agentic -count=1` — OK  
- `go test ./...` — OK  
- `go vet ./...` — OK  
- `go build -o /tmp/gh-sr ./cmd/gh-sr` — OK  

### What to watch for
- If a deployment uses a **non-`172.30.0.1`** AWF bridge gateway, set **`GH_SR_AWF_BRIDGE_GATEWAY_IP`** on the runner (inner env) so the shim and doctor checks stay consistent.
- After deploy, **`gh sr rebuild`** (or equivalent image refresh) is needed so running container-mode runner images pick up the new `docker-wrapper.sh`.

---

**[VERDICT]** Modified AWF agent `host.docker.internal` injection in `internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh` (bridge IP + `GH_SR_AWF_BRIDGE_GATEWAY_IP`); tests in `internal/runner/container_test.go`, `internal/agentic/container_network_test.go`; diagnostics in `internal/agentic/agentic.go`, `internal/doctor/doctor.go`; docs `docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`; Dockerfile comment. **Build:** OK. **Tests:** OK (`go test ./...`). **Watch:** non-default AWF gateways need `GH_SR_AWF_BRIDGE_GATEWAY_IP`; rebuild runner image for the fix to apply in production.

<timestamp>Tuesday, May 5, 2026, 10:45 AM (UTC+8)</timestamp>
<user_query>
internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh now looks like too much work around and hacky. What exactly it does. Does it change the default of `gh-aw`? Is it a workaround for the `docker-in-docker` env for gh-aw?
</user_query>
I’ll break down `docker-wrapper.sh` behavior step-by-step from the code so you can see exactly which `gh-aw` behaviors it changes and which it leaves untouched.

Yes — your read is correct: it **is** a targeted compatibility/workaround layer, mostly for `gh-aw` running inside **container-mode (DinD)** runners.

### What `docker-wrapper.sh` actually does

For most commands, it does nothing special and just passes through to real Docker:

```343:349:/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh
if needs_awf_agent_bridge_host "$@"; then
    sub="$1"
    shift
    exec "$real" "$sub" --add-host=host.docker.internal:"$AWF_HOST_DOCKER_INTERNAL_IP" "$@"
fi

exec "$real" "$@"
```

It only intercepts `docker run/create` for 2 gh-aw images:

1. **`gh-aw-mcpg` (MCP gateway)**  
   - injects `--hostname gh-aw-mcpg` (non-hex hostname) so upstream container self-inspect logic is skipped in problematic DinD cases
   - supervises foreground `docker run` lifecycle (name/cidfile/cleanup on signal)
   - rewrites generated Claude MCP URLs from `host.docker.internal` to `172.30.0.1` to avoid proxy routing issues

2. **`gh-aw-firewall/agent` (AWF agent container)**  
   - injects `--add-host=host.docker.internal:<bridge-ip>` (default `172.30.0.1`, override via `GH_SR_AWF_BRIDGE_GATEWAY_IP`) if caller didn’t provide one, to keep DB/Redis/MCP host-port reachability stable in this sandbox layout

---

### Does it change `gh-aw` defaults?

**In practice, yes — but only inside this gh-sr container-mode image path.**  
It does **not** patch gh-aw source; it alters how Docker is invoked for those specific images by putting wrapper Docker first in runner PATH/sudo secure_path:

```104:111:/home/an-lee/projects/gh-sr/internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile
# gh-sr Docker CLI shim for gh-aw inside container-mode runners (see docker-wrapper.sh).
# It is NOT what isolates concurrent runners on the host — that is runner_mode: container
# (separate inner dockerd, network namespace, port 80, /tmp/gh-aw per outer container).
# The shim: (1) skips flaky gh-aw-mcpg self-inspect under inner --network host/DinD,
# (2) supervises MCP gateway docker run + cleanup, (3) injects AWF bridge gateway mapping for AWF agents.
```

So: **default gh-aw behavior is unchanged upstream**, but **behavior is intentionally adapted in this runtime**.

---

### Is it specifically a DinD workaround?

**Yes.**  
The script itself says it’s a compatibility layer for jobs running inside container-mode runners (inner dockerd/network namespace), and docs repeat that it is not the isolation mechanism but a shim for known DinD/inner-network edge cases.

If you want, I can also outline how to simplify it safely (what parts are “must keep” vs “nice to upstream/remove”).

<user_query>
Could you dig into gh-aw(https://github.com/github/gh-aw) deeper and refactor internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/docker-wrapper.sh , make it adaptive, no hardcoding, match to the gh-aw design.
</user_query>
